paper_title: Orientations of cycles in digraphs of high chromatic number and high minimum out-degree
summary: Question 1.2 is fully answered within the source paper itself by Theorem 1.3, which characterizes all orientations of cycles $C$ that must appear as subdigraphs in every digraph $D$ (without loops or parallel arcs) with $\chi(D) \geq c$ and minimum out-degree at least $\varepsilon|V(D)|$ for fixed $\varepsilon > 0$, generalizing a result of Thomassen. The paper has been peer-reviewed and published in Combinatorica 46 (2026).
search_queries: orientations cycles digraphs chromatic number minimum out-degree Koerts Moore Spirkl 2025
arXiv:2503.20045 cite follow-up oriented cycles digraphs chromatic
notes: Question 1.2 is self-contained: it is posed and fully resolved by Theorem 1.3 within the same paper. The paper was accepted by Combinatorica (DOI: 10.1007/s00493-026-00207-0, accepted 10 February 2026, published 4 March 2026). A related arXiv preprint 2509.07760 (Xue, 2025) studies chromatic profiles of oriented cycles but was not confirmed to cite or extend Question 1.2 specifically.
